name: Hank Williams
abstract: Hiram "Hank" Williams (September 17, 1923 – January 1, 1953) was an American singer, songwriter, and musician. An early pioneer of country music, he is regarded as one of the most significant and influential musicians of the 20th century.

works_created.t: I’m So Lonesome I Could Cry
I’m So Lonesome I Could Cry

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: Hank Williams, and others in the country. I mean those are American art forms, in my opinion.

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: Nobody performed there but Country-and- Western. Roy Acuff. Hank Williams.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Christophe
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] By Hank Williams. Right. I never knew who it was by. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. So I got a lot of more back there. That's with the hillbilly singer then, isn't it?

about_quotes.speaker: Conrad "Connie" Jones
about_quotes.evidence: But yeah, I loved Hank Williams. We did Hank Williams' tune, Jambalaya.
